--- title: "Phase 10 — Bootstrap Confidence Intervals" subtitle: "Empirical parameter uncertainty by subject resampling" author: "Pramod BR" date: today format: html: theme: cosmo toc: true toc-depth: 3 code-fold: true number-sections: true fig-width: 8 fig-height: 6 execute: echo: true warning: false message: false --- ## Objectives The parameter CIs reported so far are **asymptotic** — derived from the covariance matrix under large-sample, normal assumptions. With only 12 subjects those assumptions are questionable. This phase computes **bootstrap** CIs that make no such assumption, and compares the two. ## Method — case (subject) resampling We repeatedly draw 12 subjects **with replacement** from the original 12, refit the IIV model to each resampled dataset, and collect the fixed effects. The 2.5th and 97.5th percentiles of those estimates form an empirical 95% CI. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} dat <- build_nonmem_data() fit <- load_iiv_fit() boot <- run_bootstrap(dat, nboot = 50) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` calculating covariance matrix done calculating covariance matrix done calculating covariance matrix done calculating covariance matrix done calculating done calculating covariance matrix done calculating covariance matrix done calculating covariance matrix done calculating covariance matrix done calculating covariance matrix done ``` ::: ::: **50 of 50** replicates converged (100%). A convergence rate below 100% is normal — some resamples produce awkward datasets (e.g. the same subject drawn several times) — and the rate itself is a useful stability check. ## Asymptotic vs bootstrap 95% CI ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} boot_summary(boot, fit) |> kbl(caption = "Point estimate with bootstrap median and 95% percentile interval", digits = 3) |> kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
Point estimate with bootstrap median and 95% percentile interval
Parameter Estimate Boot median Boot 2.5% Boot 97.5%
ka 1.475 1.441 1.135 2.417
cl 2.787 2.806 2.399 3.297
v 32.010 31.981 29.248 35.285
add.sd 0.261 0.237 0.043 0.476
prop.sd 0.136 0.141 0.058 0.193
````` ::: ::: Read this against the asymptotic CIs from Phase 4. **Agreement** between the two reassures us the asymptotic SEs were trustworthy; a **wider or skewed** bootstrap interval (most likely for **Ka**, the least precise parameter) reveals uncertainty the formula understated. ## Bootstrap distributions ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} boot_plot(boot) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![Bootstrap sampling distributions of CL, V, and Ka (dashed = 95% percentile interval).](09_Bootstrap_Report_files/figure-html/boot-fig-1.png){width=100%} ::: ::: ## Key takeaways - Parameter uncertainty was quantified **empirically** by subject resampling, free of the normality assumption behind asymptotic SEs. - The bootstrap and asymptotic CIs can be compared directly; close agreement validates the reported precision, while divergence (typically for Ka) flags small-sample fragility. - With n = 12 the bootstrap is itself coarse and presented as a **demonstration of method** — exactly how it would be framed for a small study. > Next (Phase 11): a **covariate forest plot** summarising the weight effect on > CL and V with confidence intervals. ```
